panels.description: Medium shot: Baloo the bear pats his belly with a gentle, proud smile, Bagheera the panther stands beside him looking thoughtful. Jungle canopy background with dappled sunlight.

bubbles.text: There--there! That was worth a little bruise. Some day thou wilt remember me.

panels.description: Wide shot: Baloo gesturing grandly to Bagheera, Mowgli dancing around their feet. Baloo looks boisterous and protective, Bagheera watching with weary wisdom.

bubbles.text: No one then is to be feared! Have a care for my ribs, Little Brother! What is all this dancing?

panels.description: Medium close-up: Mowgli, standing on Bagheera's back, shouting excitedly with wide eyes, pulling at the panther's fur.

bubbles.text: And so I shall have a tribe of my own, and lead them through the branches all day long!

panels.description: Medium shot: Baloo has snatched Mowgli off the panther. Baloo's expression is stern and angry. High contrast lighting accentuating his heavy paws.

bubbles.text: Mowgli, thou hast been talking with the Bandar-log--the Monkey People.

panels.description: Close-up: Mowgli looking up at Baloo from between his paws, eyes full of hurt and defiance. In the background, Bagheera's eyes glint like jade in the shadows.

bubbles.text: The gray apes had pity on me. No one else cared.
